subject: DevOps
title: Observability Culture
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Observability Culture.

hook.text: The ops team added monitoring. Developers ignored it. When things broke, nobody looked.

buildup.text: Observability only works when the whole team uses it. Tools without culture are shelfware.

discovery.text: Developers should own their service's metrics. They know what matters most to measure.

twist.text: Add observability to code reviews. If a feature has no metrics, it's not done yet.

climax.text: Teams that own observability respond faster and build more reliable services over time.

punchline.text: Observability is a team habit, not a tool purchase.

quiz.question: Why must observability be a cultural practice?
options: Tools are useless without a team that actively uses them
Developers should never look at production data
